hypothesis: Incorporating stereochemical diversity metrics—specifically tracking chiral center distributions and enantiomeric ratios across generated molecules—into NaviDiv's constraint framework will reveal that standard reinforcement learning optimization disproportionately collapses stereochemical diversity before structural fingerprint-based metrics detect model collapse, and that adding stereochemistry-aware penalties to the combined constraint regime will recover this hidden diversity loss while maintaining equivalent property optimization performance compared to the current combined constraint approach.
